I listen to everything—from high-energy Phonk and hype bangers to chill study tracks and soulful classics. Spotify’s algorithm handles my diverse music taste perfectly. The personalized mixes and "Recommended Stations" always introduce me to great new music based on what I’m currently vibing with, and the UI makes it easy to jump between totally different moods. However, the amount of ads on the free tier is getting out of hand. It completely breaks the vibe when you go from a smooth, emotional.

While I’m a long-term fan and heavy user of the platform, persistent dissatisfaction has accompanied the experience on 3 main points.   Firstly the interface continues in a dated idea of how modern UI can be.  Second, the home page and the suggestions of what I might like have always offered me so much of what I have never and won’t ever listen to.  Lastly that singular option of a ridiculous voice and manner of the ai DJ.  It’s absurd and jarring.  
That said, other aspects of the algorithm are great.  Discover Weekly has given me so much over the years, so for now it remains the home of my countless playlists.

6 years after switching to Amazon music, I couldn't be happier. I'm profoundly deaf, and I use the "sound tube bar" as a gift from my missus. Spotify has given me the opportunity to explore the richness of all kinds of music genres, whether it's rock, pop, or something else. Spotify provides the lyrics in order for me to follow the beat and rhythm of the songs using my soundbar speakers. I think Spotify has done a fantastic job. Perhaps they could add an interpreter to sign for each song that deaf people like to watch and feel the vibration of the sound.
I really like being in control of whatever I want to hear. It's my choice. I am also able to listen to other's Playlists, in order to broaden my options & interests.

Spotify is honestly one of those apps that just becomes part of your daily routine without you even noticing. You can switch from music to podcasts to something new you’ve never heard before, and it all just flows naturally. It’s simple, but it doesn’t feel basic.

Spotify has a great music library and a user-friendly interface, making it easy to discover and enjoy music. However, the free version has far too many ads. The advertisements are too frequent and interrupt the listening experience, making it frustrating to use. Reducing the number of ads or spacing them out better would greatly improve the app. Overall, it's a good platform, but the excessive ads are a major downside.
